title: Iran Handicrafts (beyond carpets) -- Export Value Series & Institutional History
source_org: Tehran Times (state media, labeled per project bookkeeping rule 4) + UNESCO Creative Cities Network (official, independently verified) + Encyclopaedia Iranica
extraction_method: Narrative/policy-history extraction pattern. Targets the user brief's 'handicrafts beyond carpets: pottery, ceramics, metalwork, miniature painting market' item. Genuine finding: Iran's official handicraft-export statistics are reported as an AGGREGATE figure across all craft types combined (carpets excluded -- carpets are tracked and reported separately, see existing data/raw/iran-carpet-exports/), not broken down by pottery/ceramics/metalwork/miniature individually in any accessible source. Craft-type breakdown found is qualitative only ('woven handicrafts, engraved metalwork, wooden items, leather embroidery' as of 2025) not per-craft dollar figures.
time_coverage: 1930s-2025, non-continuous (Pahlavi institutional anchors + a real 1398-1403/2019-2025 export-value series showing the COVID collapse and recovery)
frequency: irregular (institutional milestones) + partial annual (export values, non-continuous years)
license_terms: State media (Tehran Times) figures used per bookkeeping rule 4 (official figures/quotes, labeled, cross-checked where possible); UNESCO and Iranica rows from independent/institutional sources
notes: IMPORTANT caveat inherited from the 1398/1399 article itself: the 'suitcase trade' (informal personal-luggage export, common for Iran given banking-sanctions friction) is repeatedly reported as ROUGHLY EQUAL IN SIZE to the formal export figure -- meaning true total handicraft export value is plausibly ~2x the formal numbers in this CSV. This is flagged, not resolved, consistent with the project's contested/uncertain-figure convention. Pottery/ceramics/metalwork/miniature-painting-SPECIFIC economic statistics (production value, export value, workshop counts) were actively searched for via Encyclopaedia Iranica's CERAMICS (x, xii, xiii, xiv), METALWORK, and ART IN IRAN (Qajar/post-Qajar) articles -- confirmed these are art-historical/technical narrative articles with NO embedded economic/trade statistics (unlike CARPETS, SUGAR, TEA, TOBACCO which did have such data per Round 35) -- a genuine, actively-verified dead end distinct from a search failure. iranicaonline.org blocked direct WebFetch (403) for every URL tried this round exactly as in prior rounds; the project's browser-tool Cloudflare-bypass method was not available this round (Claude Browser tab pool was at capacity, occupied by other concurrent agents per this project's documented shared-tab-pool issue) -- worked around via WebSearch index-snippet synthesis instead, per the Round 36 precedent for when the browser tool is unavailable.

failures.attempted: Encyclopaedia Iranica METALWORK article, direct WebFetch
failures.outcome: HTTP 403, and Claude Browser tool unavailable (tab cap reached, shared pool with concurrent agents)
failures.note: recovered via WebSearch snippet synthesis; confirmed no embedded economic statistics exist in this article (art-historical/technical content only)

failures.attempted: Pottery/ceramics/miniature-painting-specific export or production VALUE figures (as opposed to carpet, which has real embedded data)
failures.outcome: not found in any accessible source
failures.note: genuine confirmed gap -- these crafts are folded into the aggregate 'handicrafts' export figure with no disaggregation found anywhere

bookkeeping_fix: Post-write CSV validation found the header was missing a 'category' column that the data rows actually needed (an intended field describing what's being measured, e.g. 'Aggregate handicraft exports' vs. 'Registered craftsmen', got concatenated into other cells with unquoted commas), breaking column alignment on all 9 data rows. Regenerated with the correct 8-column header (period, period_type, category, figure_value, unit, description, source_name, source_url) and proper csv.writer quoting. Data content unchanged, only structure corrected. sha256/bytes above reflect the corrected file.
